name: Prismic

unions.name: Model
unions.comment:
This is the main user-facing type for elm-prismicio's internal state.

The `Api` is represented as `Maybe Api`, because we may not have fetched it yet.

unions.name: ModelWithApi
unions.comment:
This variation of the Model type is returned by `api`, when we know we have successfully retreived the `Api`.

It is used internally by elm-prismicio.

unions.name: Predicate
unions.comment: The type representing Prismic query predicates.

values.name: any
values.comment: Match documents having any of `values` at `fragment`.

values.name: api
values.comment:
Go and fetch the Prismic API, if it has not already been fetched. You must
start every Prismic request with this function.

values.name: at
values.comment: Match documents having `value` at `fragment`.

values.name: atL
values.comment: Match documents having a list of `values` at `fragment`.

values.name: bookmark
values.comment: Convenience function for fetching a bookmarked document.
values.type: String -> Task PrismicError ModelWithApi -> Task PrismicError ( Request, ModelWithApi )

values.name: cache
values.comment:
The `submit` `Task` returns an updated Prismic `Model` with the request and
response cached.

In your app's `update` function, you should merge this with the existing cache
using `cache`.

    update msg model =
        case msg of
            MyPrismicMsg (Ok ( response, prismic )) ->
                { model
                    | prismic =
                        cache model.prismic prismic
                }

values.name: form
values.comment: Choose a form on which to base the rest of the Prismic request.
values.type: String -> Task PrismicError ModelWithApi -> Task PrismicError ( Request, ModelWithApi )

values.name: fulltext
values.comment: Match documents with a full text search at `fragment`.

values.name: getApi
values.comment: Get the `Api` information from the `Model`.

values.name: init
values.comment:
Initialise the Prismic model with the URL for your Prismic repository. Save
this in your application's Model somewhere.

    type alias Model =
        { prismic : Prismic.Model }

    init =
        { prismic =
            Prismic.init "https://lesbonneschoses.prismic.io/api"
        }

values.name: query
values.comment:
Override a Form's default query.

See the section on `Predicate`s below for how to construct a `Predicate`.
values.type: List Predicate -> Task PrismicError ( Request, api ) -> Task PrismicError ( Request, api )

values.name: ref
values.comment: Override a Form's default ref
values.type: String -> Task PrismicError ( Request, ModelWithApi ) -> Task PrismicError ( Request, ModelWithApi )

values.name: submit
values.comment:
Submit the request.

Pass this function a `Decoder` to decode each document in the response into your
own Elm document type.
values.type: Decoder docType -> Task PrismicError ( Request, ModelWithApi ) -> Task PrismicError ( Response docType, Model )

name: Prismic.Api

unions.name: FieldType
unions.comment: The type of values for a Prismic form field.

unions.name: Ref
unions.comment: A type to disambiguate `Ref`s from other `String`s.

aliases.name: Api
aliases.comment:
The `Api` for your Prismic repository.

Your app can look things up in this if you need to (for example, to resolve
links using the bookmarks `Dict`).
aliases.type: { refs : List RefProperties, bookmarks : Dict String String, types : Dict String String, tags : List String, version : String, forms : Dict String Form, oauthInitiate : String, oauthToken : String, license : String, experiments : Experiments }

aliases.name: Experiments
aliases.comment: TODO: Experiments are not Strings. Fill out this type.
aliases.type: { draft : List String, running : List String }

aliases.name: Form
aliases.comment:
Properties representing a Prismic form.

These are used to construct a default query.
aliases.type: { method : String, enctype : String, action : String, fields : Dict String FormField, rel : Maybe String, name : Maybe String }

aliases.name: FormField
aliases.comment:
A field in a Prismic form.

These are combined to construct query parameters for the eventual Http request.
aliases.type: { fieldType : FieldType, multiple : Bool, default : Maybe String }

aliases.name: RefProperties
aliases.comment:
Properties representing a Prismic ref.

Most of the time you will be working with the `master` ref, which is added to
all requests by default.
aliases.type: { id : String, ref : Ref, label : String, isMasterRef : Bool }

aliases.name: Response
aliases.comment:
Represents a Prismic response.

This type is parameterized by `docType`, which is determined by the `Decoder`
you pass to `submit`.
aliases.type: { license : String, nextPage : Maybe String, page : Int, prevPage : Maybe String, results : List (SearchResult docType), resultsPerPage : Int, resultsSize : Int, totalPages : Int, totalResultsSize : Int, version : String }

aliases.name: SearchResult
aliases.comment:
Represents a single document in a `Response`.

This type is parameterized by `docType`, which is determined by the `Decoder`
you pass to `submit`.
aliases.type: { data : docType, href : String, id : String, linkedDocuments : List DocumentReference, slugs : List String, tags : List String, resultType : String, uid : Maybe String }

values.name: decodeApi
values.comment: Decode an `Api` from JSON.

values.name: decodeResponse
values.comment: Decode a `Response` from JSON.

name: Prismic.Document

unions.name: Decoder
unions.comment:
A value that knows how to decode Documents.

Construct a `Decoder` to pass to `submit`.

unions.name: Document
unions.comment:
Holds the Prismic document.

You will decode this into your own document type by passing a `Decoder MyDoc` to
`submit`.

unions.name: Embed
unions.comment:
Embed elements.

TODO: Consolidate Embed types?

unions.name: Link
unions.comment: Links to other documents or to the web.

unions.name: StructuredText
unions.comment:
`StructuredText` can be rendered to HTML using `structuredTextAsHtml`.

TODO: Custom rendering.

aliases.name: DocumentReference
aliases.comment: A reference to a Prismic document.
aliases.type: { id : String, uid : Maybe String, slug : String, tags : List String, linkedDocumentType : String }

aliases.name: EmbedRich
aliases.type: { authorName : String, authorUrl : String, cacheAge : String, embedUrl : String, height : Maybe Int, html : String, providerName : String, providerUrl : String, title : String, url : String, version : String, width : Int }

aliases.name: EmbedVideo
aliases.type: { authorName : String, authorUrl : String, embedUrl : String, height : Int, html : String, providerName : String, providerUrl : String, thumbnailHeight : Int, thumbnailUrl : String, thumbnailWidth : Int, title : String, version : String, width : Int }

aliases.name: ImageView
aliases.comment: Properties for a single image view.
aliases.type: { alt : Maybe String, copyright : Maybe String, url : String, dimensions : ImageDimensions }

aliases.name: ImageViews
aliases.type: { main : ImageView, views : Dict String ImageView }

aliases.name: LinkResolver
aliases.comment:
A `LinkResolver` simply converts a Prismic `DocumentReference` to a list of
`Html.Attribute`s. `structuredTextAsHtml` and friends add these attributes to
links in the text.

For example, you can use this to add `onClick` handlers to links:

    type Msg
        = NavigateTo DocumentReference

    myLinkResolver : LinkResolver Msg
    myLinkResolver docRef =
        [ Html.Attributes.href ""
        , Html.Events.onClick (NavigateTo docRef)
        ]

    view : StructuredText -> Html Msg
    view myStructuredText =
        structuredTextAsHtml myLinkResolver myStructuredText

Your `update` function would handle the `NavigateTo` message and perform the
appropriate routing.

values.name: decode
values.comment:
Begin a decoding pipeline.

    type alias MyDoc =
        { title : StructuredText }

    myDocDecoder : Decoder MyDoc
    myDocDecoder =
        decode MyDoc
            |> required "title" structuredText

values.name: decodeDocument
values.comment: Internal: Run a `Decoder` against a `Document`.
values.type: Decoder a -> Document -> Result String a

values.name: decodeDocumentJson
values.comment: Decode a `Document` from JSON.

values.name: decodeDocumentReferenceJson
values.comment: Decode a `DocumentReference` from JSON.

values.name: defaultLinkResolver
values.comment:
Adds a default `href` attribute to links:

    [ href "documents/{doc.id}/{doc.slug}" ]

values.name: getFirstImage
values.comment: Get the first image out of some `StructuredText`, if there is one.

values.name: getFirstParagraph
values.comment: Get the first paragraph out of some `StructuredText`, if there is one.

values.name: getText
values.comment: Get the contents of a single `StructuredText` element as a `String`.

values.name: getTexts
values.comment: Get the contents of a some `StructuredText` as a `String`.

values.name: getTitle
values.comment: Get the first title out of some `StructuredText`, if there is one.

values.name: group
values.comment:
Decode a group.

Groups are essentially Documents, so you pass `group` a Document `Decoder`.

Here is an example with a slice containing groups:

    type alias MyDoc =
        { slices : List Slice }

    type Slice
        = SAlbum Album
        | SBook Book

    type alias Album =
        { title : String
        , cover : ImageViews
        }

    type alias Book =
        { title : String
        , blurb : StructuredText
        }

    albumDecoder : Decoder Album
    albumDecoder =
        decode Album
            |> required "title" text
            |> required "cover" image

    bookDecoder : Decoder Book
    bookDecoder =
        decode Book
            |> required "title" text
            |> required "blurb" structuredText

    myDocDecoder : Decoder MyDoc
    myDocDecoder =
        decode MyDoc
            |> required "slices"
                (sliceZone
                    [ slice "album" (group albumDecoder)
                    , slice "book" (group bookDecoder)
                    ]
                )

values.name: image
values.comment: Decode an Image field.

values.name: labelledSlice
values.comment:
Decode a slice in a slice zone. The tagger is also passed the slice label.

TODO: custom label decoders?
values.type: String -> (Maybe String -> a -> b) -> FieldDecoder a -> SliceDecoder b

values.name: link
values.comment: Decode a Link field.

values.name: map
values.comment: Transform a decoder.

values.name: optional
values.comment: Decode a field that might be missing.
values.type: String -> FieldDecoder a -> Decoder (Maybe a -> b) -> Decoder b

values.name: required
values.comment: Decode a required field.
values.type: String -> FieldDecoder a -> Decoder (a -> b) -> Decoder b

values.name: slice
values.comment: Decode a slice in a slice zone.
values.type: String -> (a -> b) -> FieldDecoder a -> SliceDecoder b

values.name: sliceZone
values.comment:
Decode a SliceZone.

Pass this function a list of possible elements that can appear in the Slice.

    type alias MyDoc =
        { section : Section }

    type Section
        = MyContent StructuredText
        | MyImage ImageViews

    myDocDecoder : Decode MyDoc
    myDocDecoder =
        decode MyDoc
            |> required "section"
                (sliceZone
                    [ slice "theContent" MyContent structuredText
                    , slice "theImage" MyImage image
                    ]
                )

values.name: structuredText
values.comment: Decode a StructuredText field.

values.name: structuredTextAsHtml
values.comment:
Render some `StructuredText` as HTML.

You must supply a `LinkResolver` to resolve any links in the `StructuredText`.
If you don't care about this, you can use the `defaultLinkResolver`.

values.name: structuredTextBlockAsHtml
values.comment: Render a single block of `StructuredText` as HTML.

values.name: text
values.comment: Decode a Text field.
